questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word CAT?
questions.explanation: C=3, A=1, T=20. Add them together: 3 + 1 + 20 = 24. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word BED?
questions.explanation: B=2, E=5, D=4. Total: 2 + 5 + 4 = 11. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word AGE?
questions.explanation: A=1, G=7, E=5. Total: 1 + 7 + 5 = 13. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these words has the highest total value?
questions.explanation: FIG: F=6+I=9+G=7=22. HEN: H=8+E=5+N=14=27. APE: A=1+P=16+E=5=22. COW: C=3+O=15+W=23=41. DOG: D=4+O=15+G=7=26. COW has the highest value at 41. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word ICE?
If A=2, B=4, C=6, D=8, what does BAD = ?
questions.explanation: B=4, A=2, D=8. BAD = 4 + 2 + 8 = 14. Each letter equals its position doubled (A=2, B=4, C=6, D=8). Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=3, B=5, C=7, what is the value of A + B + C?
questions.explanation: A=3, B=5, C=7. A + B + C = 3 + 5 + 7 = 15. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word DOG?
If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word PIG?
questions.explanation: P=16, I=9, G=7. Total: 16 + 9 + 7 = 32. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these words has the lowest total value?
questions.explanation: BAD: B=2+A=1+D=4=7. BIG: B=2+I=9+G=7=18. BAG: B=2+A=1+G=7=10. BED: B=2+E=5+D=4=11. BID: B=2+I=9+D=4=15. BAD has the lowest value at 7. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=5, B=10, C=15, what is the value of A + B − C?
questions.explanation: A=5, B=10, C=15. A + B − C = 5 + 10 − 15 = 0. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=4, B=7, C=2, what is A × B − C?
questions.explanation: A=4, B=7, C=2. First multiply: A × B = 4 × 7 = 28. Then subtract: 28 − C = 28 − 2 = 26. Remember BODMAS — multiplication before subtraction. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word FACE?
questions.explanation: F=6, A=1, C=3, E=5. Total: 6 + 1 + 3 + 5 = 15. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=3, B=6, C=9, what is the value of (B × C) ÷ A?
questions.explanation: B=6, C=9, A=3. First multiply: B × C = 6 × 9 = 54. Then divide: 54 ÷ A = 54 ÷ 3 = 18. Remember BODMAS — brackets and multiplication before division (left to right here). Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers to speed up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word SUN?
questions.explanation: S=19, U=21, N=14. Total: 19 + 21 + 14 = 54. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word HAT?

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word CUP?

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word JAM?

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word FIG?
questions.explanation: F=6, I=9, G=7. Total: 6 + 9 + 7 = 22. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word PEN?
questions.explanation: P=16, E=5, N=14. Total: 16 + 5 + 14 = 35. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word FISH?
questions.explanation: F=6, I=9, S=19, H=8. Total: 6 + 9 + 19 + 8 = 42. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word CAKE?
questions.explanation: C=3, A=1, K=11, E=5. Total: 3 + 1 + 11 + 5 = 20. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word JUMP?
questions.explanation: J=10, U=21, M=13, P=16. Total: 10 + 21 + 13 + 16 = 60. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word KING?
If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word DRUM?
questions.explanation: D=4, R=18, U=21, M=13. Total: 4 + 18 + 21 + 13 = 56. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word LIME?
questions.explanation: L=12, I=9, M=13, E=5. Total: 12 + 9 + 13 + 5 = 39. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these words has the highest total value?
questions.explanation: BIKE: 2+9+11+5=27. HIKE: 8+9+11+5=33. LIKE: 12+9+11+5=37. MIKE: 13+9+11+5=38. PIKE: 16+9+11+5=41. PIKE has the highest value at 41. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these tree names has the lowest total value?
questions.explanation: OAK: 15+1+11=27. ELM: 5+12+13=30. ASH: 1+19+8=28. FIR: 6+9+18=33. YEW: 25+5+23=53. OAK has the lowest value at 27. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word BRAVE?
questions.explanation: B=2, R=18, A=1, V=22, E=5. Total: 2 + 18 + 1 + 22 + 5 = 48. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word LIGHT?
questions.explanation: L=12, I=9, G=7, H=8, T=20. Total: 12 + 9 + 7 + 8 + 20 = 56. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word STONE?
questions.explanation: S=19, T=20, O=15, N=14, E=5. Total: 19 + 20 + 15 + 14 + 5 = 73. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word CLOUD?
If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word RIVER?
questions.explanation: R=18, I=9, V=22, E=5, R=18. Total: 18 + 9 + 22 + 5 + 18 = 72. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these words has the highest total value?
questions.explanation: FLAME: 6+12+1+13+5=37. BLAZE: 2+12+1+26+5=46. SPARK: 19+16+1+18+11=65. EMBER: 5+13+2+5+18=43. LIGHT: 12+9+7+8+20=56. SPARK has the highest value at 65. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word RUN?
questions.explanation: R=18, U=21, N=14. Total: 18 + 21 + 14 = 53. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word DEN?
questions.explanation: D=4, E=5, N=14. Total: 4 + 5 + 14 = 23. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word NEST?
questions.explanation: N=14, E=5, S=19, T=20. Total: 14 + 5 + 19 + 20 = 58. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word WOLF?
questions.explanation: W=23, O=15, L=12, F=6. Total: 23 + 15 + 12 + 6 = 56. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word QUEEN?
questions.explanation: Q=17, U=21, E=5, E=5, N=14. Total: 17 + 21 + 5 + 5 + 14 = 62. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which of these words has the highest total value?
If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word FROST?
questions.explanation: F=6, R=18, O=15, S=19, T=20. Total: 6 + 18 + 15 + 19 + 20 = 78. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of the word MAP?
questions.explanation: M=13, A=1, P=16. Total: 13 + 1 + 16 = 30. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... how much more is the word STOP worth than the word STEP?
questions.explanation: STOP: S=19+T=20+O=15+P=16 = 70. STEP: S=19+T=20+E=5+P=16 = 60. Difference: 70 − 60 = 10. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=3 and B=8, what is the value of A + B?
questions.explanation: A=3, B=8. A + B = 3 + 8 = 11. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=2, B=5 and C=9, what is the value of A + B + C?
questions.explanation: A=2, B=5, C=9. A + B + C = 2 + 5 + 9 = 16. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=6 and B=4, what is the value of A × B?
questions.explanation: A=6, B=4. A × B = 6 × 4 = 24. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=8, B=3 and C=5, what is the value of A + B − C?
questions.explanation: A=8, B=3, C=5. A + B = 8 + 3 = 11. Then 11 − C = 11 − 5 = 6. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=5, B=6 and C=3, what is the value of A × B + C?
questions.explanation: A=5, B=6, C=3. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × B = 5 × 6 = 30. Then add: 30 + C = 30 + 3 = 33. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=9, B=4 and C=6, what is the value of A × B − C?
questions.explanation: A=9, B=4, C=6. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × B = 9 × 4 = 36. Then subtract: 36 − C = 36 − 6 = 30. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=7, B=3 and C=2, what is the value of (A − B) × C?
questions.explanation: A=7, B=3, C=2. Brackets first: A − B = 7 − 3 = 4. Then multiply: 4 × C = 4 × 2 = 8. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition when there are mixed operations. ✓

questions.question: If A=8 and B=2, what is the value of A ÷ B + 5?
questions.explanation: A=8, B=2. By BODMAS, divide first: A ÷ B = 8 ÷ 2 = 4. Then add: 4 + 5 = 9. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition. ✓

questions.question: If A=4, B=11 and C=3, what is the value of B − A + C?
questions.explanation: A=4, B=11, C=3. B − A = 11 − 4 = 7. Then 7 + C = 7 + 3 = 10. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=6 and B=5, what is the value of A × B − A?
questions.explanation: A=6, B=5. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × B = 6 × 5 = 30. Then subtract: 30 − A = 30 − 6 = 24. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=3, B=5 and C=2, what is the value of A × (B + C)?
questions.explanation: A=3, B=5, C=2. Brackets first: B + C = 5 + 2 = 7. Then multiply: A × 7 = 3 × 7 = 21. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=2, B=4, C=6 and D=3, what is the value of A × B + C ÷ D?
questions.explanation: A=2, B=4, C=6, D=3. By BODMAS, do multiplication and division first: A × B = 2 × 4 = 8 and C ÷ D = 6 ÷ 3 = 2. Then add: 8 + 2 = 10. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=2, B=3 and C=10, what is the value of C − A × B?
questions.explanation: A=2, B=3, C=10. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × B = 2 × 3 = 6. Then subtract: C − 6 = 10 − 6 = 4. Remember, multiplication comes before subtraction. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice! ✓

questions.question: If A=12, B=4 and C=7, what is the value of A ÷ B + C?
questions.explanation: A=12, B=4, C=7. By BODMAS, divide first: A ÷ B = 12 ÷ 4 = 3. Then add: 3 + C = 3 + 7 = 10. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=7 and B=9, what is the value of 2 × A + B?
questions.explanation: A=7, B=9. By BODMAS, multiply first: 2 × A = 2 × 7 = 14. Then add: 14 + B = 14 + 9 = 23. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers to speed up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=4, C=8 and D=5, what is the value of (A + B) × (C − D)?
questions.explanation: A=1, B=4, C=8, D=5. First bracket: A + B = 1 + 4 = 5. Second bracket: C − D = 8 − 5 = 3. Then multiply: 5 × 3 = 15. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A=10, B=3 and C=2, what is the value of A − B × C?
questions.explanation: A=10, B=3, C=2. By BODMAS, multiply first: B × C = 3 × 2 = 6. Then subtract: A − 6 = 10 − 6 = 4. Be careful — multiplication comes before subtraction! Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=4, B=2 and C=8, what is the value of (C + A) ÷ B?
questions.explanation: A=4, B=2, C=8. Brackets first: C + A = 8 + 4 = 12. Then divide: 12 ÷ B = 12 ÷ 2 = 6. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=5 and B=7, what is the value of A × A − B?
questions.explanation: A=5, B=7. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × A = 5 × 5 = 25. Then subtract: 25 − B = 25 − 7 = 18. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=3, B=2, C=5 and D=4, what is the value of A × C − B × D?
questions.explanation: A=3, B=2, C=5, D=4. By BODMAS, do both multiplications first: A × C = 3 × 5 = 15 and B × D = 2 × 4 = 8. Then subtract: 15 − 8 = 7. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition. ✓

questions.question: If A=12 and B=5, what is the value of A − B?
questions.explanation: A=12, B=5. A − B = 12 − 5 = 7. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=4 and B=3, what is the value of B × B + A?
questions.explanation: A=4, B=3. By BODMAS, multiply first: B × B = 3 × 3 = 9. Then add: 9 + A = 9 + 4 = 13. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=10, B=6 and C=2, what is the value of (A − B) × (A − C)?
questions.explanation: A=10, B=6, C=2. First bracket: A − B = 10 − 6 = 4. Second bracket: A − C = 10 − 2 = 8. Then multiply: 4 × 8 = 32. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=8, B=3 and C=1, what is the value of A − B − C?
questions.explanation: A=8, B=3, C=1. A − B = 8 − 3 = 5. Then 5 − C = 5 − 1 = 4. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=3, B=8 and C=3, what is the value of B × C ÷ A?
questions.explanation: A=3, B=8, C=3. By BODMAS, work left to right for multiplication and division: B × C = 8 × 3 = 24. Then 24 ÷ A = 24 ÷ 3 = 8. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A=4 and B=6, what is the value of A + B + A?
If A=5, B=2 and C=4, what is the value of A × B + A × C?
questions.explanation: A=5, B=2, C=4. By BODMAS, do both multiplications first: A × B = 5 × 2 = 10 and A × C = 5 × 4 = 20. Then add: 10 + 20 = 30. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice! ✓

questions.question: If A=9 and B=2, what is the value of A × B + B?
questions.explanation: A=9, B=2. By BODMAS, multiply first: A × B = 9 × 2 = 18. Then add: 18 + B = 18 + 2 = 20. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=6, B=2 and C=5, what is the value of (A + C) × B − C?
questions.explanation: A=6, B=2, C=5. Brackets first: A + C = 6 + 5 = 11. Then multiply: 11 × B = 11 × 2 = 22. Finally subtract: 22 − C = 22 − 5 = 17. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A=7, B=4 and C=3, what is the value of A + B × C?
questions.explanation: A=7, B=4, C=3. By BODMAS, multiply first: B × C = 4 × 3 = 12. Then add: A + 12 = 7 + 12 = 19. Be careful — multiplication comes before addition! Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers to speed up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: The same number fills both gaps. What is it? 2 × ___ + ___ = 27
questions.explanation: Call the missing number n. Then 2 × n + n = 3 × n = 27. So n = 27 ÷ 3 = 9. Check: 2 × 9 + 9 = 18 + 9 = 27. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A = 2, B = 5 and C = 3, what is the value of A + B + C?
questions.explanation: A + B + C = 2 + 5 + 3 = 10. Tip: Double-check by adding the numbers in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A = 6 and B = 9, what is the value of B − A?
questions.explanation: B − A = 9 − 6 = 3. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A = 4 and B = 7, what is the value of A + A + B?
If A = 3, B = 8 and C = 1, what is the value of B − A − C?
questions.explanation: B − A − C = 8 − 3 − 1 = 4. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5 (A-E=1-5, F-J=6-10, K-O=11-15, P-T=16-20, U-Z=21-26). ✓

questions.question: If A = 5, B = 10 and C = 2, what is the value of B − A + C?
questions.explanation: B − A + C = 10 − 5 + 2 = 7. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick letter counting! ✓

questions.question: If A = 3, B = 7 and C = 4, what is the value of B × C + A?
questions.explanation: B × C + A = 7 × 4 + 3. By BODMAS, multiply first: 7 × 4 = 28. Then add: 28 + 3 = 31. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A = 8, B = 2 and C = 5, what is the value of A ÷ B + C?
questions.explanation: A ÷ B + C = 8 ÷ 2 + 5. By BODMAS, divide first: 8 ÷ 2 = 4. Then add: 4 + 5 = 9. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers to speed up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A = 6, B = 3 and C = 9, what is the value of C − A × B?
questions.explanation: C − A × B = 9 − 6 × 3. By BODMAS, multiply first: 6 × 3 = 18. Then subtract: 9 − 18 = −9. Remember, multiplication comes before subtraction! Tip: Double-check by adding in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A = 5 and B = 4, what is the value of A × B − B?
questions.explanation: A × B − B = 5 × 4 − 4. By BODMAS, multiply first: 5 × 4 = 20. Then subtract: 20 − 4 = 16. Tip: For comparison Qs, estimate first — late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A = 7, B = 3 and C = 6, what is the value of (A + B) × C?
questions.explanation: Brackets first: A + B = 7 + 3 = 10. Then multiply: 10 × C = 10 × 6 = 60. Tip: For comparison questions, estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A = 11 and B = 3, what is the value of A × B − A?
questions.explanation: A × B − A = 11 × 3 − 11. By BODMAS, multiply first: 11 × 3 = 33. Then subtract: 33 − 11 = 22. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiply before you add. ✓

questions.question: If A = 9, B = 4 and C = 2, what is the value of A − B × C?
questions.explanation: A − B × C = 9 − 4 × 2. By BODMAS, multiply first: 4 × 2 = 8. Then subtract: 9 − 8 = 1. Be careful — multiplication comes before subtraction! Tip: Write intermediate totals — don't hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A = 15, B = 5 and C = 8, what is the value of A ÷ B + C?
questions.explanation: A ÷ B + C = 15 ÷ 5 + 8. By BODMAS, divide first: 15 ÷ 5 = 3. Then add: 3 + 8 = 11. Tip: Learn the alphabet in groups of 5. ✓

questions.question: If A = 3, B = 5, C = 2 and D = 4, what is the value of A × B − C × D?
questions.explanation: A × B − C × D = 3 × 5 − 2 × 4. By BODMAS, do both multiplications first: 3 × 5 = 15 and 2 × 4 = 8. Then subtract: 15 − 8 = 7. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice! ✓

questions.question: If A = 4, B = 6 and C = 2, what is the value of (A + B) × (B − C)?
questions.explanation: First bracket: A + B = 4 + 6 = 10. Second bracket: B − C = 6 − 2 = 4. Then multiply: 10 × 4 = 40. Tip: Write down intermediate totals — don't try to hold everything in your head. ✓

questions.question: If A = 2, B = 3, C = 6 and D = 4, what is the value of C × D ÷ A + B?
questions.explanation: C × D ÷ A + B = 6 × 4 ÷ 2 + 3. By BODMAS, do multiplication and division left to right: 6 × 4 = 24, then 24 ÷ 2 = 12. Then add: 12 + 3 = 15. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A = 5, B = 8 and C = 3, what is the value of B × B − A × C?
questions.explanation: B × B − A × C = 8 × 8 − 5 × 3. By BODMAS, do both multiplications first: 8 × 8 = 64 and 5 × 3 = 15. Then subtract: 64 − 15 = 49. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers to speed up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A = 7, B = 2, C = 3 and D = 5, what is the value of (A − B) × C + D?
questions.explanation: Brackets first: A − B = 7 − 2 = 5. Then multiply: 5 × C = 5 × 3 = 15. Then add: 15 + D = 15 + 5 = 20. Tip: Watch out for double letters — count them twice (BELL = B+E+L+L)! ✓

questions.question: If A = 2, B = 9, C = 4 and D = 3, what is the value of B × C ÷ D + A?
questions.explanation: B × C ÷ D + A = 9 × 4 ÷ 3 + 2. By BODMAS, multiplication and division left to right: 9 × 4 = 36, then 36 ÷ 3 = 12. Then add: 12 + 2 = 14. Tip: Double-check by adding in a different order. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which two words have the same total value? ACE, DAD, BIG, HEN, FIG
questions.explanation: ACE = A(1)+C(3)+E(5) = 9. DAD = D(4)+A(1)+D(4) = 9. BIG = B(2)+I(9)+G(7) = 18. HEN = H(8)+E(5)+N(14) = 27. FIG = F(6)+I(9)+G(7) = 22. ACE and DAD both equal 9! Tip: Calculate ALL words before comparing — don't stop early! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which two words have the same total value? STONE, PLANE, MOUSE, TRAIN, TIGER
questions.explanation: STONE = S(19)+T(20)+O(15)+N(14)+E(5) = 73. PLANE = P(16)+L(12)+A(1)+N(14)+E(5) = 48. MOUSE = M(13)+O(15)+U(21)+S(19)+E(5) = 73. TRAIN = T(20)+R(18)+A(1)+I(9)+N(14) = 62. TIGER = T(20)+I(9)+G(7)+E(5)+R(18) = 59. STONE and MOUSE both equal 73! Tip: Calculate ALL words before comparing — don't stop early! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the difference in value between CAT and DOG?
questions.explanation: CAT = 24. DOG = 26. Difference = 26 - 24 = 2. Tip: Always subtract the smaller from the larger! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the difference in value between FISH and BIRD?
questions.explanation: FISH = 42. BIRD = 33. Difference = 42 - 33 = 9. Tip: Always subtract the smaller from the larger! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the difference in value between HOME and LAND?
questions.explanation: HOME = 41. LAND = 31. Difference = 41 - 31 = 10. Tip: Always subtract the smaller from the larger! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the difference in value between SMILE and FROWN?
questions.explanation: SMILE = 58. FROWN = 76. Difference = 76 - 58 = 18. Tip: Always subtract the smaller from the larger! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the difference in value between TIGER and MOUSE?
questions.explanation: TIGER = 59. MOUSE = 73. Difference = 73 - 59 = 14. Tip: Always subtract the smaller from the larger! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is E - B?
questions.explanation: E = 5, B = 2. So E - B = 5 - 2 = 3. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is H - C?
questions.explanation: H = 8, C = 3. So H - C = 8 - 3 = 5. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is G - D?
questions.explanation: G = 7, D = 4. So G - D = 7 - 4 = 3. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is M - F?
questions.explanation: M = 13, F = 6. So M - F = 13 - 6 = 7. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is P - H?
questions.explanation: P = 16, H = 8. So P - H = 16 - 8 = 8. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is T - K?
questions.explanation: T = 20, K = 11. So T - K = 20 - 11 = 9. Tip: Use EJOTY anchors (E=5, J=10, O=15, T=20, Y=25) for quick counting! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which word has the highest total value? SUN, RAIN, SNOW, WIND, HAIL
questions.explanation: SNOW=71, SUN=54, WIND=50, RAIN=42, HAIL=30. SNOW has the highest value at 71! Tip: Estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters (S,T,U,V,W) score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... which word has the highest total value? GRAPE, LEMON, PEACH, MANGO, PLUM
questions.explanation: PLUM=62, LEMON=59, MANGO=50, GRAPE=47, PEACH=33. PLUM has the highest value at 62! Tip: Estimate first — words with late-alphabet letters (S,T,U,V,W) score higher. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of RHYTHM?
questions.explanation: R(18) + H(8) + Y(25) + T(20) + H(8) + M(13) = 92. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of WIZARD?
questions.explanation: W(23) + I(9) + Z(26) + A(1) + R(18) + D(4) = 81. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is the total value of MONEY?
questions.explanation: M(13) + O(15) + N(14) + E(5) + Y(25) = 72. Tip: Add in pairs that make round numbers — it speeds up mental arithmetic! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is C × D?
questions.explanation: C(3) × D(4) = 12. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is E × C?
questions.explanation: E(5) × C(3) = 15. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is B × F?
questions.explanation: B(2) × F(6) = 12. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is D × E - C?
questions.explanation: D(4) × E(5) = 20, then 20 - C(3) = 17. Remember BODMAS — multiply first! Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is G × B + D?
questions.explanation: G(7) × B(2) = 14, then 14 + D(4) = 18. Multiply before adding! Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is F × C - E?
questions.explanation: F(6) × C(3) = 18, then 18 - E(5) = 13. BODMAS: multiply first! Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is H × B - C?
questions.explanation: H(8) × B(2) = 16, then 16 - C(3) = 13. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is E × D + B?
questions.explanation: E(5) × D(4) = 20, then 20 + B(2) = 22. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is C × C × B?
questions.explanation: C(3) × C(3) = 9, then 9 × B(2) = 18. Tip: Remember BODMAS! Multiplication before addition/subtraction. ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is F + G - D?
questions.explanation: F(6) + G(7) = 13, then 13 - D(4) = 9. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is J - E + C?
questions.explanation: J(10) - E(5) = 5, then 5 + C(3) = 8. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is H + D - F?
questions.explanation: H(8) + D(4) = 12, then 12 - F(6) = 6. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is K - G + E?
questions.explanation: K(11) - G(7) = 4, then 4 + E(5) = 9. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is T - J + E - B?
questions.explanation: T(20) - J(10) = 10, + E(5) = 15, - B(2) = 13. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is M + H - K + C?
questions.explanation: M(13) + H(8) = 21, - K(11) = 10, + C(3) = 13. Tip: Work left to right, writing down each intermediate total! ✓

questions.question: If A=1, B=2, C=3... what is O - F + D - A?
questions.explanation: O(15) - F(6) + D(4) - A(1) = 12. Work left to right: 15-6=9, 9+4=13, 13-1=12. Tip: Write down intermediate totals! ✓
